vineyards.name: Krug
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only through the Krug hospitality programme; the family house is not open to the general public.
vineyards.description: Prestige Reims maison founded in 1843, owned by LVMH, built on a multi-vintage reserve blend across all three main grapes for its Grande Cuvee.
vineyards.tip: Each Grande Cuvee release carries an ID code linking it to its blend and disgorgement.

vineyards.name: Moet & Chandon
vineyards.tasting_program: Guided cellar tours and tastings in French or English; book in advance, the cellars reopened after renovation.
vineyards.description: The largest Champagne house, founded 1743 in Epernay and owned by LVMH, with 28 km of chalk cellars on the Avenue de Champagne and the Dom Perignon prestige cuvee.
vineyards.tip: The Dom Perignon prestige label is produced here and released only in declared vintages.

vineyards.name: Louis Roederer
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment; the family house focuses on private and trade hospitality.
vineyards.description: Family-owned Reims maison founded 1776, famous for the Cristal prestige cuvee created for Tsar Alexander II and for its estate-owned, partly biodynamic vineyards.
vineyards.tip: Cristal is made only from estate vineyards, much of them farmed biodynamically.

vineyards.name: Champagne Bollinger
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment; Pinot-led house known for barrel fermentation and long lees ageing.
vineyards.description: Family-owned Ay house founded 1829, Pinot Noir-led with oak fermentation and a reserve of magnums; long the supplier of fictional spy James Bond's Champagne.
vineyards.tip: The rare Vieilles Vignes Francaises comes from ungrafted, pre-phylloxera Pinot Noir vines.

vineyards.name: Champagne Pol Roger
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment; family house on the renamed Epernay street, formerly rue Henri Lelarge.
vineyards.description: Family-owned Epernay maison founded 1849, Winston Churchill's favourite Champagne, with cool, deep cellars and a prestige cuvee named in his honour.
vineyards.tip: The street was renamed rue Winston Churchill in 2025; older sources still say rue Henri Lelarge.

vineyards.name: Veuve Clicquot Ponsardin
vineyards.address: 1 place des Droits de l'Homme, 51100 Reims, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Guided cellar tours and tastings; booking required, with a Clicquot Cafe on site.
vineyards.description: Reims house founded 1772 and owned by LVMH, shaped by Madame Clicquot, who pioneered the riddling table; known for its Yellow Label brut.
vineyards.tip: Madame Clicquot is credited with inventing the riddling rack and the first known vintage rose.

vineyards.name: Perrier-Jouet
vineyards.tasting_program: Belle Epoque Society tastings and visits at Maison Belle Epoque; reservation required.
vineyards.description: Epernay house founded 1811, owned by Pernod Ricard, known for the Art Nouveau anemone bottle of its Belle Epoque prestige cuvee and a Chardonnay-leaning style.
vineyards.tip: The anemone bottle was designed by Emile Galle in 1902 and revived for Belle Epoque in 1969.

vineyards.name: Champagne Taittinger
vineyards.tasting_program: Guided tours of the Gallo-Roman chalk crayeres at Saint-Nicaise; booking recommended in season.
vineyards.description: Reims house tracing to 1734 and re-acquired by the Taittinger family in 2006, Chardonnay-led with deep Gallo-Roman chalk cellars beneath the Saint-Nicaise hill.
vineyards.tip: Its cellars occupy former Gallo-Roman chalk pits and the crypt of a 13th-century abbey.

vineyards.name: Ruinart
vineyards.tasting_program: Tours of the UNESCO-listed chalk crayeres, tastings and garden visits at the redesigned 4 rue des Crayeres site.
vineyards.description: The oldest established Champagne house, founded 1729 in Reims and owned by LVMH, Chardonnay-focused and famous for its deep UNESCO-listed chalk crayeres.
vineyards.tip: Its crayeres descend about 40 metres into Gallo-Roman chalk and are a UNESCO World Heritage feature.

vineyards.name: Champagne Billecart-Salmon
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate visits by appointment at Mareuil-sur-Ay; family house known for cold, slow fermentation.
vineyards.description: Family-owned Mareuil-sur-Ay house founded 1818, celebrated for its delicate brut rose and a long, cold fermentation that yields a fresh, precise style.
vineyards.tip: Its salmon-pink Brut Rose is one of the most acclaimed roses in Champagne.

vineyards.name: Charles Heidsieck
vineyards.tasting_program: The house is not generally open to the public for visits.
vineyards.description: Reims house founded 1851 by Charles-Camille Heidsieck and owned by EPI, prized for a large reserve-wine component and rich, mature Brut Reserve.
vineyards.tip: Founder Charles-Camille earned the nickname Champagne Charlie selling in 19th-century America.

vineyards.name: Piper-Heidsieck
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings; book in advance.
vineyards.description: Reims house founded 1785 and owned by EPI, Pinot-led with a crisp, fruit-forward brut and the rare prestige cuvee Rare.
vineyards.tip: Marilyn Monroe famously name-checked the brand; the prestige cuvee Rare is now its own label.

vineyards.name: Champagne Laurent-Perrier
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment; family group house at Tours-sur-Marne.
vineyards.description: Family-controlled house at Tours-sur-Marne founded 1812, known for a fresh, Chardonnay-influenced style and a pioneering saignee Cuvee Rose.
vineyards.tip: Its Cuvee Rose is made by skin maceration (saignee), unusual among the big houses.

vineyards.name: Champagne Deutz
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment at the Ay house.
vineyards.description: Ay house founded 1838 by William Deutz and Pierre-Hubert Geldermann, now part of the Roederer group, with an elegant, structured Pinot-led style.
vineyards.tip: Its prestige cuvee Amour de Deutz is a refined Chardonnay-driven counterpart to the William Deutz.

vineyards.name: Champagne Salon
vineyards.address: 5-7 rue de la Breche d'Oger, 51190 Le Mesnil-sur-Oger, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Not open to the general public; allocation only through trade and private channels.
vineyards.description: Single-cuvee Blanc de Blancs house at Le Mesnil-sur-Oger, founded 1905, releasing only declared vintages of Cuvee S from one Grand Cru village.
vineyards.tip: Salon makes one wine only and in non-declared years the fruit goes to sister house Delamotte.

vineyards.name: Champagne Philipponnat
vineyards.address: 13 rue du Pont, Mareuil-sur-Ay, 51160 Ay-Champagne, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment; house known for its walled single vineyard Clos des Goisses.
vineyards.description: Mareuil-sur-Ay house founded 1910, owner since 1935 of Clos des Goisses, the steep south-facing walled vineyard behind one of Champagne's great single-clos wines.
vineyards.tip: Clos des Goisses is among the steepest and warmest sites in Champagne, so it is rarely chaptalised.

vineyards.name: Champagne Henriot
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment in Reims; book ahead in high season.
vineyards.description: Family Reims house founded 1808 by Apolline Henriot, Chardonnay-leaning with a fresh, refined style and the prestige Cuve des Enchanteleurs.
vineyards.tip: The house has a strong Cote des Blancs sourcing, giving its blends a Chardonnay backbone.

vineyards.name: Champagne Lanson
vineyards.tasting_program: Year-round guided tours of the Clos Lanson, winery and cellars; reservations required.
vineyards.description: Reims house founded 1760 and part of Lanson-BCC, known for a fresh, malolactic-free style and the walled Clos Lanson vineyard in the city.
vineyards.tip: Lanson traditionally blocks malolactic fermentation, keeping its wines crisp and ageworthy.

vineyards.name: G.H. Mumm
vineyards.address: 34 rue du Champ de Mars, 51100 Reims, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours and tastings daily; book in advance, cellars span around 25 km.
vineyards.description: Reims house founded 1827 and owned by Pernod Ricard, Pinot-led with the red-sash Cordon Rouge brut and around 25 km of city cellars.
vineyards.tip: The Cordon Rouge red sash references the French Legion of Honour ribbon.

vineyards.name: Champagne Gosset
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits to the Epernay house and its classified one-hectare park, open to the public since 2021.
vineyards.description: The oldest wine house in Champagne, established at Ay in 1584 and now based in Epernay, owned by Renaud-Cointreau and known for blocking malolactic fermentation.
vineyards.tip: Gosset began as a still-wine producer in 1584, long before sparkling Champagne existed.

vineyards.name: Domaine Jacques Selosse
vineyards.tasting_program: No general visits; the family also runs the Hotel Restaurant Les Avises in Avize. Allocation only.
vineyards.description: Cult Avize grower under Anselme Selosse, applying Burgundian, low-intervention and oxidative methods to terroir-driven Blanc de Blancs and lieu-dit cuvees.
vineyards.tip: Selosse pioneered grower-Champagne terroir thinking; bottles are scarce and allocation-only.

vineyards.name: Champagne Egly-Ouriet
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits only by prior arrangement, subject to availability.
vineyards.description: Benchmark Ambonnay grower under Francis Egly, working old Grand Cru Pinot Noir at low yields with very long lees ageing for powerful, vinous Champagne.
vineyards.tip: Les Vignes de Vrigny is a rare 100 percent Pinot Meunier Premier Cru bottling.

vineyards.name: Champagne Pierre Peters
vineyards.address: 26 rue des Lombards, 51190 Le Mesnil-sur-Oger, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower house; visits by appointment in Le Mesnil-sur-Oger.
vineyards.description: Leading Le Mesnil-sur-Oger Chardonnay grower under Rodolphe Peters, working Grand Cru vines with a perpetual reserve and the single-vineyard Les Chetillons.
vineyards.tip: Les Chetillons is a single-vineyard Le Mesnil Blanc de Blancs from old Chardonnay vines.

vineyards.name: Champagne Pascal Agrapart
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits by appointment in Avize.
vineyards.description: Avize Chardonnay grower under Pascal Agrapart, farming Cote des Blancs Grand Cru chalk with low-intervention methods for taut, terroir-marked Blanc de Blancs.
vineyards.tip: Cuvees like Mineral and Terroirs spotlight specific Grand Cru parcels of the Cote des Blancs.

vineyards.name: Champagne Larmandier-Bernier
vineyards.address: 19 avenue du General de Gaulle, 51130 Vertus, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits and tastings by appointment in Vertus.
vineyards.description: Vertus grower farming the Cote des Blancs since 1971, organic from 1992 and Demeter-certified biodynamic from 2003, making vivid, low-dosage Blanc de Blancs.
vineyards.tip: Terre de Vertus is a non-dose single-village Blanc de Blancs from Premier Cru Vertus.

vineyards.name: Champagne Vilmart & Cie
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower house; visits by appointment in Rilly-la-Montagne.
vineyards.description: Rilly-la-Montagne grower founded 1890, known for oak-fermented, Chardonnay-rich Premier Cru cuvees of depth and texture such as Coeur de Cuvee.
vineyards.tip: Vilmart ferments in large oak casks, unusual for a Montagne de Reims grower.

vineyards.name: Champagne Chartogne-Taillet
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits by appointment in Merfy.
vineyards.description: Merfy grower with roots to 1683, led by Alexandre Chartogne toward terroir-driven, single-parcel Champagnes from the northern Massif de Saint-Thierry.
vineyards.tip: Les Barres comes from ungrafted, own-rooted old Pinot Meunier vines.

vineyards.name: Champagne Ulysse Collin
vineyards.tasting_program: Small grower; no public tasting room, allocation through importers.
vineyards.description: Congy grower founded 2003 by Olivier Collin in the Sezannais, making single-vineyard, lieu-dit Champagnes of texture and depth from reclaimed family vines.
vineyards.tip: Les Pierrieres is a single-vineyard Blanc de Blancs from chalky Sezannais soils.

vineyards.name: Roses de Jeanne (Cedric Bouchard)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tiny grower; no public visits, allocation only.
vineyards.description: Celles-sur-Ource micro-grower founded 2000 by Cedric Bouchard in the Cote des Bar, bottling single-parcel, single-vintage, single-grape Champagnes with no dosage.
vineyards.tip: Each cuvee is one parcel, one grape and one vintage, with no added dosage.

vineyards.name: Champagne La Closerie (Jerome Prevost)
vineyards.address: 65 rue des Dames de France, 51390 Gueux, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Tiny grower; visits by email appointment only, subject to availability.
vineyards.description: Gueux micro-grower founded 1998 by Jerome Prevost, with a cult following for Meunier-dominant single-vineyard Champagne from the Les Beguines parcel.
vineyards.tip: Les Beguines is built almost entirely on old-vine Pinot Meunier, rare as a varietal cuvee.

vineyards.name: Champagne Frederic Savart
vineyards.tasting_program: Small grower; visits by appointment in Ecueil.
vineyards.description: Ecueil grower under Frederic Savart on the south Montagne de Reims, making precise, Pinot-led Premier Cru Champagnes from massale-selection vines.
vineyards.tip: Savart works the prized pinot fin d'Ecueil, an old massale selection of Pinot Noir.

vineyards.name: Champagne Marguet
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits by appointment in Ambonnay.
vineyards.description: Ambonnay grower led by Benoit Marguet, farming Grand Cru vines biodynamically with horse ploughing, Demeter-certified, for vivid low-dosage Champagnes.
vineyards.tip: Marguet has ploughed its vineyards with horses since 2010, unusual in Champagne.

vineyards.name: Champagne Bereche et Fils
vineyards.address: 33 rue Craon de Ludes, 51500 Ludes, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits by appointment in Ludes.
vineyards.description: Ludes grower established 1847, run by brothers Raphael and Vincent Bereche toward low-dosage, terroir-driven Champagnes with extended lees ageing.
vineyards.tip: Le Cran is a single-village Ludes Premier Cru that helped build the estate's modern reputation.

vineyards.name: Champagne Drappier
vineyards.tasting_program: Family estate in the Cote des Bar; visits and tastings, see the house for hours.
vineyards.description: Family house at Urville in the Cote des Bar, founded 1808, Pinot-led and a custodian of Champagne's rare grapes through its four-grape Quattuor.
vineyards.tip: Quattuor blends the rare Arbane, Petit Meslier and Blanc Vrai with Chardonnay.

vineyards.name: Champagne Pierre Gimonnet & Fils
vineyards.address: 1 rue de la Republique, 51530 Cuis, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower reception and tasting in Cuis, Monday to Friday and Saturday by appointment.
vineyards.description: Cuis Chardonnay grower bottling since 1935, with major Cote des Blancs holdings including Cramant and Chouilly, making crisp, precise Blanc de Blancs.
vineyards.tip: The family owns more Cote des Blancs vineyard than almost any other grower.

vineyards.name: Champagne Paul Bara
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits by appointment in Bouzy.
vineyards.description: Seventh-generation Bouzy grower founded 1833, working 100 percent Grand Cru vines, Pinot Noir-led with a renowned rose and the rare still Bouzy Rouge.
vineyards.tip: All the fruit comes from Grand Cru Bouzy, famed for ripe, powerful Pinot Noir.

vineyards.name: Champagne Vazart-Coquart & Fils
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits by appointment in Chouilly.
vineyards.description: Chouilly Grand Cru Chardonnay grower founded 1954 under Jean-Pierre Vazart, making generous, single-village Blanc de Blancs with a large perpetual reserve.
vineyards.tip: All fruit comes from Grand Cru Chouilly at the northern end of the Cote des Blancs.

vineyards.name: Champagne Diebolt-Vallois
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits by appointment in Cramant.
vineyards.description: Cramant Chardonnay grower making Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs from the Cote des Blancs, with the old-vine, oak-fermented prestige cuvee Fleur de Passion.
vineyards.tip: Fleur de Passion is fermented in old oak from old vines and made without malolactic.

vineyards.name: Champagne Gaston Chiquet
vineyards.address: 912 avenue du General Leclerc, 51530 Dizy, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Grower estate; visits by appointment in Dizy.
vineyards.description: Dizy grower bottling estate Champagne since the 1930s, with the rare Blanc de Blancs d'Ay from Chardonnay grown in the Pinot stronghold of Ay.
vineyards.tip: Its Blanc de Blancs d'Ay is a rare Chardonnay grown in a village famed for Pinot Noir.
